response.justification: Bogdan Szabo is a capable full-stack engineer with a broad background, but he is a poor fit for this role. His C++ experience is limited to approximately six months at National Instruments (2014–2015) working on a C IDE, plus a bachelor thesis project — far short of the 5–10 years required. His primary professional stack is TypeScript, Node.js, and DLang. There is no evidence of Windows-platform C++ development, no fixed income or capital markets domain expertise, no SQL Server or Oracle proficiency highlighted, and no mention of Summit, Kafka, or MQ. His brief stint at RIDE Capital involved investment practices but in a Node/TypeScript capacity unrelated to capital markets systems.
strengths: Demonstrated software craftsmanship discipline (TDD, CI/CD, trunk-based development) applicable to any engineering context
Some C++ exposure at National Instruments on LabWindows/CVI, showing ability to work in compiled, performance-sensitive environments
Strong communication skills evidenced by public talks, workshops, and open-source community involvement
concerns: C++ professional experience is under one year and does not meet the 5–10 year mandatory requirement
No fixed income, fixed income derivatives, investment banking, or capital markets domain knowledge
No demonstrated Windows-platform C++ work, SQL Server/Oracle proficiency, or financial systems (Summit, messaging frameworks) exposure

key_factors.factor: No capital markets / fixed income domain
